code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1 evidence analysis only

reliability.note: Coverage boundary well defined; one gap: no explicit rule for when figure images cannot be viewed (e.g., file format not supported)

performance_context.note: 271 lines with seven reference modules; appropriate

human_usability.note: Sample triggers are conversational and natural; excellent discoverability for researchers wanting a fast paper read

agent_specific.note: Good scope boundaries and overinterpretation check mechanism; description is information-dense and trigger-rich

inputs.label: Full paper with figures provided — standard figure-first read

assertions.text: Figure-to-claim table present with figure, shown content, claim, support judgment
Observed content separated from attached interpretation per figure
Support strength classified as Strong/Partial/Weak/Does not establish
1-3 core figures identified as carrying the paper's main claims
Overinterpretation and narrative stretch check applied

inputs.label: Multi-panel figures with different panels supporting different claims

assertions.text: Multi-panel figure decomposed into separable evidence units
Different panels not treated as one undifferentiated block
Panel-level claims assessed separately
Panels supporting different claim types (descriptive vs mechanistic) distinguished
No fabricated panel labels, p-values, or numeric results

inputs.label: Paper with visually impressive figures but weak evidentiary support

assertions.text: Visually striking figures correctly not equated with strong evidence
Narrative overclaim identified where visual support is weak
Weakest figures explicitly identified with reason
True takeaway reflects visual support level, not narrative persuasion
Figure captions not used as proxy for evidence strength

inputs.label: Only captions provided — no actual figure images available

assertions.text: Read correctly labeled as caption-based, not figure-based
No visual content inferred beyond what captions describe
Support strength judgments marked as provisional pending figure access
User informed that full figure-first read requires actual figures
Caption-level inference clearly separated from figure content inference
assertions.note: One panel interpretation drew on likely visual conventions rather than described content — not clearly labeled as inferred

inputs.label: Large paper with 8+ figures and supplementary — identify core vs peripheral figures

assertions.text: 1-3 core figures correctly identified as carrying main claims
Supplementary figures treated as less central unless explicitly stated otherwise
Figure order logic reconstruction coherent across 8+ figures
Decorative or contextual figures distinguished from evidence-carrying figures
Self-critical risk review present

key_strengths: Panel-level decomposition of multi-panel figures is a rare and valuable feature for complex modern biomedical papers
Five support-strength classifications (Strong/Partial/Weak/Does not establish/Ambiguous) provide precise and actionable evidentiary judgment
Overinterpretation-check rules module explicitly addresses the most common figure-to-claim inflation patterns
Caption-level vs figure-level coverage distinction prevents overconfident reads from insufficient input

recommendations.title: Caption-only mode allows inferred panel interpretations without explicit labeling
recommendations.problem: When only captions are available, one panel interpretation was based on likely visual conventions rather than described content, without being explicitly labeled as inferred.
recommendations.root_cause: The coverage boundary rules allow caption-based reconstruction but do not define the boundary between 'inferable from caption' and 'assumed based on visual convention'.
recommendations.fix: Add a rule: in caption-only mode, any interpretation that goes beyond the caption text must be labeled '[INFERRED from caption — verify against actual figure]'.

recommendations.title: No explicit handling rule when figure images cannot be viewed due to format issues
recommendations.problem: The skill handles missing figures and caption-only scenarios, but does not specify behavior when figures are provided in a format that cannot be processed (e.g., vector graphics, certain PDF embedded images).
recommendations.root_cause: Input validation focuses on content availability but not format-level access.
recommendations.fix: Add a format accessibility check: if figure content cannot be extracted or viewed, apply the same caption-only protocol and inform the user that figure images are inaccessible in the current format.

recommendations.title: Skill does not offer clear handoff to methods or reliability appraisal after figure-first read
recommendations.problem: The skill correctly notes it does not replace full methods appraisal, but does not provide a natural handoff to a dedicated methods or evidence reliability skill.
recommendations.fix: Add a brief downstream routing note at the end of Section G: 'For full methods evaluation, use methods-reverse-engineer. For full evidence quality rating, use evidence-level-ranker or medical-research-literature-reader-pro.'
